basic_details.job_title: Chhattisgarh High Court DEO Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 38 Data Entry Operator Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Chhattisgarh High Court, Bilaspur has officially released Advertisement No. 01/II-14-1/2026 (DEO) for recruitment to the post of Data Entry Operator (DEO). Online applications are invited through the Chhattisgarh Vyapam website at https://vyapamcg.cgstate.gov.in/ from eligible candidates having the requisite qualifications. A total of 38 posts have been announced across various categories. The post carries a pay scale of Level-6 of pay matrix (₹25,300 – ₹80,500). This advertisement/recruitment and appointment shall be subject to the final outcome of the S.L.P. (C) No. 19668/2022 of the Sup

important_dates.exam_date: The exam is tentatively scheduled for Sunday, June 28, 2026, from 10:00 AM to 12:15 PM in Bilaspur and Raipur
important_dates.admit_card_date: Admit cards are tentatively set to be released on the Vyapam website on Monday, June 22, 2026.

application_fee.details: The application fee details for Chhattisgarh High Court DEO Recruitment 2026 will be as notified on the official Chhattisgarh Vyapam portal at https://vyapamcg.cgstate.gov.in/. | Candidates are advised to visit the official Vyapam website for complete and updated fee-related information before submitting their application. All fee payment must be done through the prescribed online mode on the Vyapam portal only. No TA/DA will be admissible to any candidate for appearing in any of the tests conducted as part of the selection process.

age_limit.age_details: 21 to 30 years (for non-Chhattisgarh residents); 21 to 40 years (for bonafide residents of Chhattisgarh) as on 01.01.2026
age_limit.details: The age limit for Chhattisgarh High Court DEO Recruitment 2026 is calculated as on 01.01.2026. The minimum age is 21 years and the maximum age is 30 years for general/non-Chhattisgarh resident candidates. For bonafide residents of Chhattisgarh, the maximum age limit is 40 years.

qualification.education_qualification: Second class Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science or related subject; OR Second class Bachelor's Degree with PGDCA; OR Second class Bachelor's Degree with 'O' level course from DOE
qualification.experience: Working knowledge of Operating Systems and Office application suites
qualification.details: Before applying for Chhattisgarh High Court DEO Recruitment 2026, candidates must ensure they satisfy all the prescribed eligibility conditions. The candidate must be a citizen of India. Working knowledge of Unix/Open Source Software/Windows NT is required along with the following educational qualifications. | The primary educational qualification is at least a second class Bachelor's Degree from a recognised University in Computer Science or a related subject; OR a second class Bachelor's Degree from any recognised University with PGDCA from any recognised University; OR a second class Bachelor's Degree from a recognised University with 'O' level course from DOE (Department of Electronics).

vacancy_details.Post Reserved (PwD): 04 Posts reserved for Physically Disabled Persons (OA, OL, OAL, BL, HH, LV, OH, Acid Attack Victim, Muscular Dystrophy, Dwarfism, Leprosy Cured, Autism)

category_wise_vacancy.Post Reserved (PwD): 04 Posts reserved for Physically Disabled Persons (OA, OL, OAL, BL, HH, LV, OH, Acid Attack Victim, Muscular Dystrophy, Dwarfism, Leprosy Cured, Autism)

salary_details.details: Selected candidates appointed to the post of Data Entry Operator (DEO) at the Chhattisgarh High Court, Bilaspur will be entitled to pay as per Level-6 of the Chhattisgarh Government Pay Matrix. In addition to the basic pay, candidates will be entitled to all other allowances and benefits as per the rules applicable to State Government employees. Selected candidates after their joining will be kept on probation for the period as mentioned in the applicable rules.

selection_process: Section I: 60 Marks
Reasoning Quantitative Aptitude English General Studies & General Knowledge
15 Marks 15 Marks 15 Marks 15 Marks
Section II: 40 Marks - Computer
Unix/Open Source Software NT/Operating System, MS Windows & Ubuntu, Word Processing, Spreadsheet, PowerPoint, Access, Working with LAN, Antivirus, Network and Internet
Test Speed Requirement Marks
Computer Typing Test - Hindi 250 words in 10 minutes 25 Marks
Computer Typing Test - English 300 words in 10 minutes 25 Marks
Total 50 Marks

The selection process for Chhattisgarh High Court DEO Recruitment 2026 consists of two phases - Phase I Written Test and Phase II Skill Test. The examination is conducted through the Chhattisgarh Vyapam portal. Phase I Written Test is only a screening test; the merit list will be prepared exclusively on the basis of marks obtained in Phase II Skill Test
and the marks obtained in Phase I Written Test will not be considered for the preparation of the Merit List/Select List.
Candidates will be called for Phase II Skill Test in the ratio of 1:15 based on marks obtained in the Written Test. After receipt of the merit list (category wise - SC
ST
OBC
Male
Female and Divyang separately
and common merit list) from Vyapam
the High Court will publish the same on its website and document verification will be conducted at the High Court. After document verification
the select list and waiting list will be published on the official website of the Chhattisgarh High Court. In case of tie in marks in the Skill Test
the candidate born earlier will be placed above. If date of birth is also the same
preference will be given to candidates who have passed the Typewriting examination in Hindi and English from any recognised board.
Phase I - Written Test (100 Marks)
The Written Test consists of 100 Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) divided into two sections. There shall be no negative marking in Phase I.
Phase II - Skill Test (50 Marks)
The Skill Test consists of Hindi and English Typing Tests conducted in Ubuntu Linux – Libre Office – Unicode Font. A deduction of 1/4 mark will be made for each mistake. To qualify
the candidate must secure a minimum of 10 marks in each typing test out of 25.
how_to_apply: Eligible candidates must apply online only through the official Chhattisgarh Vyapam website at https://vyapamcg.cgstate.gov.in/. No other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must ensure they fulfil all the eligibility criteria before applying. Candidates should apply only if the conditions mentioned in the advertisement are acceptable to them. Candidates already in service must produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer at the time of document verification; failure to produce NOC will result in rejection of candidature. Reserved category candidates (SC/ST/OBC) must submit a permanent Caste Certificate duly issued by the competent authority along with a certificate of residence of Chhattisgarh at the time of document verification. Reserved candidates (SC/ST/OBC) are also required to submit an affidavit at the time of joining in the proforma prescribed by the State Government with memo dated 29.06.2013, failing which their appointment shall be cancelled.
Visit the official Chhattisgarh Vyapam website at https://vyapamcg.cgstate.gov.in/
Register and fill the online application form with accurate personal, qualification, and category details
Pay the application fee as per the prescribed mode on the Vyapam portal
Submit the application and retain a copy for future reference
Bring all original documents along with self-attested copies at the time of document verification
important_instructions: Candidates applying for Chhattisgarh High Court DEO Recruitment 2026 must carefully read and comply with all conditions mentioned in the official advertisement. The number of posts may increase or decrease, and the number of reserved posts may also vary at any time. This advertisement/recruitment and appointment shall be subject to the final outcome of the S.L.P. (C) No. 19668/2022 of the Supreme Court of India. The Competent Authority reserves the right to cancel, withdraw, or modify the entire or any part of the recruitment process at any stage without assigning any reason.

No TA/DA will be admissible to any candidate for appearing in any of the tests. Candidates with less than 40% disability will not be considered under the Physically Disabled (Divyang) Category. Only those candidates who fulfil and accept all conditions mentioned in the advertisement should apply for the said post.

Posts reserved for SC, ST, and OBC are for bonafide residents of Chhattisgarh only; other state SC/ST/OBC candidates will be considered under UR posts only. OBC candidates must not belong to the creamy layer. Women reservation is applicable only to local residents of Chhattisgarh. Candidates already in service must produce NOC from their employer at document verification. Physically Disabled candidates with less than 40% disability will not be considered under PwD category. No TA/DA will be admissible for appearing in any test. Merit list will be prepared only on the basis of Phase II Skill Test marks; Phase I Written Test marks will not be used for merit list preparation. The Competent Authority reserves the right to cancel, withdraw, or modify the recruitment process at any stage without assigning any reason. Recruitment is subject to the final outcome of S.L.P. (C) No. 19668/2022 of the Supreme Court of India.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in Chhattisgarh High Court DEO Recruitment 2026 and what is the pay scale?
faq.answer: The Chhattisgarh High Court, Bilaspur has notified 38 vacancies for the post of Data Entry Operator (DEO) with a pay scale of Level‑6 of the Pay Matrix, ₹25,300 – ₹80,500, plus admissible state government allowances as per rules.

faq.question: Q2. What are the online application dates and exam date for Chhattisgarh High Court DEO 2026?
faq.answer: Online applications through the Chhattisgarh Vyapam portal open on 20 April 2026 and close on 12 May 2026, with an error‑correction window from 13 to 15 May 2026 up to 5:00 PM. The written exam is tentatively scheduled on Sunday, 28 June 2026 from 10:00 AM to 12:15 PM at centres in Bilaspur and Raipur, and admit cards are expected to be released on 22 June 2026.

faq.question: Q3. How are the 38 DEO vacancies distributed category‑wise and what about PwD reservation?
faq.answer: Out of 38 posts, 19 are Unreserved (including 5 for women), 7 for SC (including 2 women), 7 for ST (including 2 women) and 5 for OBC (including 1 woman). A total of 4 posts are horizontally reserved for Persons with Disabilities (PwD/Divyang) in categories such as OA, OL, OAL, BL, HH, LV, Muscular Dystrophy, Dwarfism, Leprosy Cured, Autism and Acid Attack Victim, as per state government norms.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for Chhattisgarh High Court DEO posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must have at least a second class Bachelor’s Degree from a recognised University in Computer Science or a related subject, OR a second class Bachelor’s Degree with PGDCA from a recognised University, OR a second class Bachelor’s Degree with ‘O’ level course from DOE (Department of Electronics), along with working knowledge of Unix/Open Source Software/Windows NT and office application suites.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit and relaxation for Chhattisgarh High Court DEO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: As on 01.01.2026, the age limit is 21 to 30 years for General/non‑Chhattisgarh residents, and 21 to 40 years for bonafide residents of Chhattisgarh. SC/ST/OBC candidates of Chhattisgarh get 5 years relaxation, local women candidates get 10 years, government servants get an additional 5 years, and PwD candidates get relaxation as per GAD circular dated 27.09.2014, subject to an overall maximum age of 45 years including all relaxations.

faq.question: Q6. Is typing knowledge compulsory for Chhattisgarh High Court DEO posts?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates must have knowledge of Hindi and English typing, and preference is given to those who have passed a typewriting examination in both Hindi and English from a recognised board, which is particularly important for the Phase II Skill Test and tie‑breaking in the final merit list.

faq.question: Q7. How many candidates will be called for the DEO Skill Test and how is the merit list prepared?
faq.answer: Candidates will be called for Phase II Skill Test in a 1:15 ratio against category‑wise vacancies based on their Phase I Written Test scores. After the Skill Test, a category‑wise merit list (SC/ST/OBC/UR, male/female and Divyang) will be prepared only on the basis of Skill Test marks, and in case of a tie, the older candidate (by date of birth) is ranked higher; if date of birth is also same, preference goes to those who have passed Hindi and English typewriting examinations from a recognised board.

faq.question: Q8. How can candidates apply online for Chhattisgarh High Court DEO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Eligible candidates must apply online only at vyapamcg.cgstate.gov.in, by registering on the Vyapam portal, filling the DEO application form with accurate personal, educational and category details, uploading required documents, paying the prescribed application fee (as notified on the Vyapam website), and submitting the form before 12 May 2026; a printout of the application should be retained for future reference and document verification.
